articleSummary: The article argues that array- and set-based programming simplifies data handling by treating all inputs as arrays and prioritizing higher-order functions over traditional class-backed models. It critiques common object-oriented anti-patterns like bloated schema, fragile instance state, and transactional complexity, advocating instead for array-centric methods that reduce boilerplate and improve flexibility (e.g., Java’s `List<Post>` handling). The tone is analytical and slightly polemical, using Java code examples to demonstrate how array-based approaches can unify singular and bulk operations. Framed as a rebuttal to "schema surplusage," it targets developers entrenched in OOP patterns, urging a shift toward functional, set-oriented thinking inspired by SmallTalk principles.
